Name: Vicomte Alexandre du Lyon
Family:
Wife: Vicomtesse Geneviève, known for her sharp tongue and political savvy.
Children: None, which is a point of contention and gossip.
Parents: Father was a renowned knight, died in battle. Mother manages family estates with an iron fist.
Extended Family: Numerous cousins, many of whom serve in various capacities at court.
Occupation: Vicomte and court advisor. Often involved in the intricacies of court politics and intrigue.
Personality:
Outwardly: Charismatic but untrustworthy. Known for his duplicitous nature.
Inwardly: Highly ambitious, often at the expense of others. Views relationships as opportunities for advancement.
Social Status: Well-connected due to his family's history, but his reputation for deceit undermines his standing.
Potential Illness: Occasionally suffers from insomnia, driven by his constant plotting and scheming.
Body Condition: Average height, with a lean build. His appearance is always calculated to impress or intimidate.
Hobbies and Interests:
Enjoys playing complex board games, often drawing parallels to real-life strategies.
Frequent attendee of the theatre, appreciating the art of deception and performance.
An amateur poet, though his works often have hidden barbs or messages.
Skilled in dance, often using balls and social events to his advantage.
Style of Talking:
Speaks with a smooth, persuasive tone.
Often uses flattery or insinuation to manipulate conversations.
Adept at speaking in riddles or double meanings, leaving his true intentions obscured.
Unique Quirks:
Carries a locket with a portrait of an unknown woman, fueling rumors about his past.
Has an uncanny ability to recall minor details about people, using this to his advantage.
Secrets or Scandals:
Suspected of having a hand in a rival's downfall at court.
Whispers of a secret alliance with a foreign power, though no proof has been found.
